projectDescription: A Java implementation of a family tree data structure that supports parsing tree definitions from files, traversing ancestor chains, and finding the most recent common ancestor between two nodes.
keyFeatures: Parse family tree definitions from structured text files
Build a tree with parent-child relationships using an inner TreeNode class
Traverse ancestor chains from any node up to the root
Find the most recent common ancestor (MRCA) of two named individuals
GUI file chooser for selecting input files via Swing
Custom TreeException for input validation and error handling
publicApiSurface: FamilyTree() — constructor that opens a file chooser and parses the selected tree file
getMostRecentCommonAncestor(String name1, String name2) — returns the deepest common ancestor node
toString() — returns a human-readable indented representation of the tree
TreeNode.getName() — returns the name stored in a tree node
TreeNode.addChild(TreeNode childNode) — adds a child and sets its parent pointer
TreeNode.getNodeWithName(String targetName) — recursively searches the subtree for a named node
TreeNode.collectAncestorsToList() — returns an ordered list of ancestors from parent to root
TreeException(String s) — custom exception for malformed input or missing names
